---
title: Home
---

# Thinking with Data

## Exploratory Data Analysis & Visualization

*A field guide to exploring the world through data.*

Welcome to **DATA 110: Exploratory Data Analysis & Visualization**.

In this course, you'll learn to ask meaningful questions, explore real datasets, create compelling visualizations, and communicate your findings clearly using Python.

Whether we're investigating exoplanets, engineering systems, climate records, or election polls, we'll return to the same five-step process:

> **Question → Explore → Visualize → Interpret → Reflect**

---

## Start Here

If you're new to the course, begin with the **Start Here** page.

There you'll find:

- the philosophy behind the course
- how the flipped classroom works
- what you'll learn this semester
- what to expect each week

➡️ [Start Here](start_here.qmd)

---

## This Week

*(This section will be updated throughout the semester.)*

**Current topic**

Week 1 — What is Data Science?

**Before class**

- Watch the introductory video
- Complete the Canvas pre-class quiz

**In class**

- First Google Colab notebook
- Exploring our first dataset

---

## Course Navigation

📅 [Schedule](schedule.qmd)

📖 [Field Guide](modules.qmd)

💻 [Assignments](assignments.qmd)

🧰 [Resources](resources.qmd)

📋 [Course Policies](course_policies.qmd)

---

## Course Philosophy

This course is not primarily about learning Python.

It is about learning to think with data.

Programming gives us powerful tools, but curiosity, critical thinking, visualization, and clear communication are the skills that will stay with you long after the semester ends.
